'''Jacob Alick-Wiencke''' (born 3 November 1999) is a [[Papua New Guinea national rugby league team|Papua New Guinea]] international [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|sr}}er or {{rlp|lf}} for the [[Leigh Leopards]] in the [[Super League]].<ref>{{Cite web |date=2022-06-16 |title=Titans debutant called up to Kumuls |url=https://www.titans.com.au/news/2022/06/16/titans-debutant-called-up-to-kumuls/ |access-date=2022-10-19 |website=Gold Coast Titans |language=en}}</ref>

==Early life==
Alick-Wiencke was born in [[Brisbane]], [[Australia]]. His late grandmother was born in [[Daru]], [[Papua New Guinea]].<ref>{{cite web|date=23 June 2022|title=PNG Kumuls debutant, Jacob Alick to play for Grandmother|url=https://news.pngfacts.com/2022/06/png-kumuls-debutant-jacob-alick-to-play.html|work=Papua New Guinea Today|access-date=28 May 2023}}</ref>

==Career==
Alick-Wiencke made his international debut for [[Papua New Guinea national rugby league team|Papua New Guinea]] in their 24-14 victory over [[Fiji national rugby league team|Fiji]] in the 2022 Pacific Test. In round 19 of the [[2023 NRL season]], Alick made his first grade debut for the Gold Coast against the [[Dolphins (NRL)|Dolphins]].<ref>{{Cite web |title=Papua New Guinea v Fiji |url=https://www.nrl.com/draw/internationals/2022/round-1/papua-new-guinea-v-fiji/ |access-date=2022-10-19 |website=National Rugby League |language=en}}</ref> Alick represented PNG at the [[2021 Men's Rugby League World Cup|2021 World Cup]] held in 2022.<ref>{{cite web | url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2022/10/03/johnston-coates-among-key-faces-in-kumuls-world-cup-squad/ | title=Johnston, Coates among key faces in Kumuls World Cup squad | date=3 October 2022 }}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://nothingbutleague.com/2023/07/09/2023-nrl-round-19-dolphins-23-titans-21/|title=2023 NRL ROUND 19: DOLPHINS 23, TITANS 21|website=nothingbutleague.com}}</ref>
Alick played a total of five matches for the Gold Coast in the [[2023 NRL season]] as the club finished 14th on the table.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.sportingnews.com/au/rugby-league/news/nrl-2023-gold-coast-titans-season-review/nel398u2vn7faognqvwnq7g4#:~:text=Did%20the%20Titans%20over%2Ddeliver,out%20of%20the%20finals%20picture./|title=NRL 2023: Gold Coast Titans season review|website=www.sportingnews.com|date=5 September 2023 }}</ref>

In round 12 of the [[2024 NRL season]], he played his first NRL match of 2024 in the [[Gold Coast Titans]] upset win over the Brisbane Broncos 36-34, he had 9 runs for 100m, a try assist, 27 tackles playing at centre. He played six games throughout 2024 as the club finished 14th on the table.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.nine.com.au/sport/nrl/news-2024-the-mole-season-review-gold-coast-titans-analysis-exclusive-20240908-p5k8xf.html/|title=The Mole's end-of-season review: Titans 'perennial enigma' who went missing as Des Hasler 'hallmark' falls flat|website=www.nine.com.au}}</ref> On 19 November 2024, the Titans announced that Alick-Wiencke had re-signed with the club for the 2025 season.<ref>{{Cite web |author=Staff Writer |date=2024-11-19 |title=PNG International extends time on the Coast |url=https://www.titans.com.au/news/2024/11/19/png-international-extends-time-on-the-coast/ |access-date=2024-11-19 |website=Gold Coast Titans |language=en}}</ref>

=== 2025 ===
Alick-Wiencke was released by Gold Coast at the end of their season.<ref>{{Cite web |date=2025-09-16 |title=Titans thank departing players in '25 |url=https://www.titans.com.au/news/2025/09/16/titans-thank-departing-players-in-25/ |access-date=2025-09-16 |website=Gold Coast Titans |language=en}}</ref>

On 7 October 2025 it was reported that he had signed for [[Leigh Leopards]] in the [[Super League]] on a 3-year deal<ref>{{cite web|website=Total RL|date=7 Oct 2025|author=Callum Walker|title=Gold Coast Titans forward Jacob Alick-Wiencke makes Leigh Leopards move|url=https://www.totalrl.com/gold-coast-titans-forward-jacob-alick-wiencke-makes-super-league-move/}}</ref>

On 12 October 2025 he made his debut for the [[PNG Prime Minister's XIII]] in the 28-10 defeat to Australia’s [[Prime Minister's XIII]] in Port Moresby<ref>{{cite web|website=FoxSports.au|date=12 Oct 2025|author=Daniel Nuttall|title=Zero for seven?! Aussie’s double hat-trick powers PM’s XII past PNG despite horrific kicking|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/rugby-league-2025-aus-pm-xiii-v-png-pm-xiii-teams-where-to-watch-start-time-kevin-walters-mitch-moses-rugby-league-news/news-story/b7b5454aadeb76356a021e7e9bbcefa3}}</ref>
